journal_entry.description: Refinance bank loan - old note replaced with new note
journal_entry.rationale: Refinancing transaction extinguishes the old loan liability and establishes a new loan liability. The debit to the old loan (liability decrease, slot d=1) removes the $100,000 obligation, and the credit to the new loan (liability increase, slot a=1) records the $100,000 new obligation. This is a balance sheet-only transaction with no P&L impact. No tax implications as loan refinancing is not a taxable event.

output.reason: Refinancing replaces one loan with another. The old loan is paid off (liability decrease), which is recorded as a debit to the old loan liability. The credit side records the creation of the new loan = liability increase.

credit_classifier.reason: Refinancing replaces one loan with another. The old loan is paid off (liability decrease), which is recorded as a debit to the old loan liability. The credit side records the creation of the new loan = liability increase.

output.reason: Refinancing replaces an old loan with a new loan. On the debit side, we eliminate (decrease) the old liability by debiting the old loan payable. The new loan is established on the credit side (liability increase). This is 1 liability decrease.
